name: Fish Pate
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Trout, or other firm fleshed fish
2 sprigs Thyme OR 1/2 tsp Dried Thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Filet the fish and place it in a marinade made of shallots, lime juice, white wine, thyme, bay leaf, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Let marinate for an hour.
2. Grind all but two of the fillets. Cook the remaining fillets in the marinade until just done, about 6 minutes. Remove the fillets and reduce the marinade by half.
3. Heat milk, butter, and salt in a saucepan. Add flour, all at once.
4. Beat vigorously until the mixture forms a ball. Beat in egg yolks off the heat, one at a time.
5. Add 1/4 cup of ground fish to a food processor and whirl the machine. Then add 1/4 cup of the mixture and a small piece of ice. When half of the fish and mixture are used, add eggs and an egg yolk. Continue alternating the mixture, fish, and ice.
6. Add salt, pepper, cayenne, then slowly add cream while the machine is running.
7. Add nutmeg and butter, and finish with more butter and tarragon.
8. Line a terrine with buttered paper (the chef used butter wrappers) and fill it with half the ground fish mixture.
9. Place the fillets on top of the mixture and cover with the remaining mixture. Put the terrine in a larger pan and add water halfway up the terrine.
10. Cook in a 350°F oven for 45 minutes or until the mousse is puffed and brown.
11. Invert, trim, and serve in slices with sauce.
Sauce:
- Chop crabs into rough pieces and cook them in butter with shallots, thyme, and parsley until they turn color.
- Add wine, salt, pepper, cayenne, and cream. Heat to boiling, add the bay leaf, and reduce until thick.
- Strain into another pan and reduce again until the consistency of whipping cream.
